description: Central, no-hardcode configuration for the private Data Function App, its private endpoint, APIM import, and Foundry agent tool wiring. Consumed by scripts/deploy-function-app.ps1, scripts/configure-function-apim.ps1, scripts/create_function_agent.py and config/__init__.py.
comment_hosting_plan_quota: eastus App Service quota was 0 ('Total VMs') on first attempt but is now available (confirmed 2026-06-23): F1, B1, B2, B3 all create successfully in eastus. Using B1 (cheapest tier supporting regional VNet integration + Always On + inbound private endpoint; F1 Free supports none of those).

hosting_plan.comment: All pre-existing App Service plans are in West US 2; private VNet integration requires the plan in East US, so reuse is not viable. eastus Basic quota confirmed available -- B1 is the smallest tier that supports VNet integration, Always On, and a private endpoint. Set reuse_existing=true and reuse_plan_name only if an East US plan already exists.
hosting_plan.name: asp-fdryvnetgw-data-eastus

apim.api_description: Private Azure Function data APIs (product catalog, inventory, orders) exposed through the private APIM AI gateway for consumption by an Azure AI Foundry agent.

entra_auth.comment: Managed-identity (Entra ID) auth for the data-function API. The APIM API-scope policy runs validate-azure-ad-token (checks aud + tenant + oid) when an Authorization header is present, else falls back to an ip-filter for dev hosts. The Foundry agent's managed identity presents a token for 'audience'. This replaces fragile IP allowlisting for the agent call path. See function-app/README.md > 'Managed-identity auth for the Foundry agent'.

ip_allowlist.comment: Azure /16 ranges covering this developer's rotating SNAT egress pool (see function-app/README.md > Developer Access). Used as the dev fallback (otherwise branch) on the data-function API and as the API-scope ip-filter on the sensitive APIs. NOTE: APIM ip-filter does NOT accept CIDR -- it requires explicit address ranges.

ip_filter_apis.comment: Other APIs on the same APIM instance that must keep their ip-filter protection after it is removed from the global scope. scripts/configure-apim-mi-auth.ps1 injects the ip_allowlist ranges into each at API scope (idempotent, preserves existing policy).

foundry_agent.name: Data-Function-Agent
foundry_agent.instructions: You are an operations assistant for Contoso. Use the data_function_api tool to fetch live product catalog, inventory, and order data. Always call the tool to answer questions about products, categories, stock levels, or orders instead of guessing. Cite the productId or orderId you used. If the tool returns no data, say so explicitly.

endpoints.comment: Populated/echoed by the deployment scripts. Static values here are the deterministic names derived from the resource names above.
